extraction.one_line_summary: DE GHENT Thomas won Stage 2 from a 4h14m breakaway, while MATTHEWS Michael took the field sprint 13+ minutes later.
tactical_patterns: De Ghent's breakaway gained 13.5+ minutes on flat stage, suggesting peloton miscalculated early stage dynamics completely.
Matthews positioned far back in sprint chaos, yet still won from distance suggesting superior power over positioning.

rider_signals.DE GHENT Thomas: positive: Dominated 4h14m solo breakaway on terrain typically controlled by sprinter teams, revealing underestimation of his TT power
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Still not at finish 13.5min after winner, crashed on consecutive stages suggesting poor positioning habits
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Won field sprint despite being caught behind crash positioning, indicating exceptional peak power compensates for tactical errors
rider_signals.MCCARTHY Jay: negative: Crashed hitting kerb in sprint approach, revealing poor road furniture awareness in technical finishes
